slides.title: Methods and Properties 🛠️
slides.content:
# Instance Methods

Instance methods are functions that belong to an object and can access its properties.

**Getters and setters** are special methods that allow you to define computed properties or add logic when accessing or modifying a property.

quizData.question: What is a getter used for?

options.text: To retrieve a property value, possibly with computation

quizData.explanation: A getter retrieves a value, often computed from other properties.

slides.title: Using Getters and Setters
slides.content: Define a `Rectangle` class with a getter for area:

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Prefer getters over methods for simple computations

If a property can be computed quickly and doesn't take arguments, use a getter for cleaner syntax.
